steps.title: Balance value and limitation
steps.content: The passage first presents The Fifty-Year Mission as a large, ambitious oral history of Star Trek that includes accounts from cast and crew members across the franchise. Then it identifies a shortcoming: the work lacks a clear authorial point of view to unify the accounts.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A invents a comparison between television and film coverage; the passage says the work includes both. Choice B says key events are missing, but the stated limitation is not missing information. Choice C claims many people did not participate, which the passage never says.

steps.title: Choose the full main idea
steps.content: The main idea must include both the book's value and the problem created by its oral-history approach. A summary that calls the work a worthwhile attempt to recount the franchise thoroughly but notes an important limitation captures both halves of the passage. The answer is D.
